gene_region.label: TP53 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical TP53 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: TP53 encodes the p53 tumor suppressor, a stress-responsive transcription factor that integrates DNA damage, oncogene activation, hypoxia, ribosomal stress, and metabolic stress to control cell-cycle arrest, apoptosis, DNA repair, and senescence programs.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local TP53 bundle is tumor-suppressor and DNA-damage-response oriented. Germline pathogenic variants require clinical review for Li-Fraumeni syndrome context, while common and somatic markers should stay separated from germline risk interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: TP53 interpretation depends heavily on variant class, zygosity, tumor versus germline origin, and whether the event affects the DNA-binding domain or regulatory regions.
Common polymorphisms such as Arg72Pro are research modifiers; truncating, missense hotspot, or deletion events can have much higher clinical impact when externally classified.
condition_research_overview: Li-Fraumeni syndrome and somatic oncology context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: TP53 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for TP53.
Because TP53 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use TP53 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000141510: TP53 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: TP53 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TP53 stress-response thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to DNA-damage checkpoint tone, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, senescence, cancer cohort biology, or high-impact tumor-suppressor review depending on the exact allele class. Do not merge benign/common TP53 markers with pathogenic germline or somatic hotspot interpretation without external classification.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) is curated as a TP53 marker for Cancer susceptibility and tumor-suppressor biology, DNA damage response and apoptosis. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Li-Fraumeni syndrome and somatic oncology context
research_context: Use this marker as a TP53 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) with TP53 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) or linked TP53 locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Cancer susceptibility and tumor-suppressor biology, DNA damage response and apoptosis
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs1042522 (TP53 Arg72Pro) as a gene-specific marker for Cancer susceptibility and tumor-suppressor biology, DNA damage response and apoptosis and DNA damage checkpoint, p53 transcriptional control, apoptosis, cell-cycle arrest, and senescence.
